subject: Ecology
title: What Is Pollination?
description: A 60-second lesson on What Is Pollination?.

hook.text: Pollination is nature's delivery service—moving pollen from one flower to another.

buildup.text: Pollen carries the male genetic material. It must reach the female part to make a seed.

discovery.text: Most flowering plants can't self-pollinate and depend on external agents to move pollen.

twist.text: Without pollination, there would be no fruits, no seeds, and no next generation of most plants.

climax.text: About 87% of all flowering plant species require animal pollination to reproduce.

punchline.text: No pollen delivery, no next generation.

quiz.question: What percentage of flowering plants require animal pollination?
